content.text:
Here are the verification results for both references.

---

**Reference 1: Xie et al. (2024)**

Your citation: Xie, J., Zhang, K., Chen, J., Lou, R., & Su, Y. (2024). "Adaptive Chameleon or Stubborn Sloth: Revealing the Behavior of Large Language Models in Knowledge Conflicts." ICLR 2024.

**Verdict: CORRECT.** Title, authors, venue, and year all match. The paper was in fact an ICLR 2024 **Spotlight**, which you could optionally note. No discrepancies found.

---

**Reference 2: Longpre et al. (2021)**

Your citation: Longpre, S., Perisetla, K., Chen, A., Ramesh, N., DuBois, C., & Singh, S. (2021). "Entity-Based Knowledge Conflicts in Question Answering." EMNLP 2021.

**Verdict: CORRECT.** Title, all six authors, venue (EMNLP 2021), and year all match exactly. No discrepancies found.

---

Both references are real, published papers with accurate details as cited.

Sources:
- [arXiv: Adaptive Chameleon or Stubborn Sloth](https://arxiv.org/abs/2305.13300)
- [OpenReview: ICLR 2024](https://openreview.net/forum?id=auKAUJZMO6)
- [ACL Anthology: Entity-Based Knowledge Conflicts](https://aclanthology.org/2021.emnlp-main.565/)
- [arXiv: Entity-Based Knowledge Conflicts](https://arxiv.org/abs/2109.05052)
